'''Robert "Robbie" Mulhern''' (born 18 October 1994) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Robbie Mulhern: Hull KR sign Leeds Rhinos prop">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/35008957|title=Robbie Mulhern: Hull KR sign Leeds Rhinos prop|publisher=BBC|date=2015-12-04|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> Mulhern has represented [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.<ref name="rugbyleagueproject.org">[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/robbie-mulhern/summary.html RLP]</ref>

He has previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at the [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Mulhern also played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and on loan from [[Hull KR]] at [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].

==Background==
Mulhern was born in Pontefract, West Yorkshire, England.

==Early career==
As a junior he played for the Castleford Panthers, Normanton Knights and he spent time in the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]' Academy System.

==Senior career==
=== Leeds Rhinos ===
Mulhern made only five appearances for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] during 2014 and 2015.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

=== Hunslet Hawks ===
Mulhern was loaned to the [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] in the 2015 [[rugby league]] season. He made twenty appearances and scored two tries.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===
Mulhern joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] ahead of the 2016 [[Super League]] season. Mulhern suffered relegation from the [[Super League]] with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the 2016 season, due to losing the [[Million Pound Game]] by the [[Salford Red Devils]].{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}
[[File:Rob Mulhern.jpg|thumb|right|Mulhern in action for Hull KR in 2017]]
12-months later however, Mulhern was part of the [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' side that won promotion back to the [[Super League]], at the first time of asking following relegation the season prior. It was revealed on 1 May 2018, that Mulhern had signed a new long-term contract, to remain at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] until at least the end of the 2021 [[rugby league]] season.

=== Warrington Wolves ===
It was announced on 10 December 2020 that Mulhern would be joining [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in a two-year swap deal with [[Luis Johnson]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55253311|title=Robbie Mulhern: Warrington sign Hull KR prop as Luis Johnson goes in opposite direction|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=13 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Leigh===
On 20 October 2022, it was announced that Mulhern had signed a contract to join the newly promoted [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12725625/leigh-leopards-promoted-side-unveil-rebrand-and-nine-new-signings-for-super-league-2023/|title=Leigh Leopards: Promoted side unveil rebrand and nine new signings for Super League 2023|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, Mulhern played for Leigh in the 2023 Challenge Cup final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Mulhern played 27 games for Leigh in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in their elimination playoff loss against Hull Kington Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./|title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review|website=www.leighleopards.co.uk}}</ref>
Mulhern played 22 games for Leigh in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish fifth on the table. He played in the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12204/13227859/super-league-semi-finals-live-wigan-face-leigh-for-grand-final-spot/|title=Super League semi-finals: Wigan Warriors cruise past Leigh Leopards- live commentary and video clips|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

==International career==
=== Ireland ===
Mulhern is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international.<ref name="Leeds Rhinos: Rising star awarded Rhinos’ final squad number">{{cite web|url=http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos/leeds-rhinos-rising-star-awarded-rhinos-final-squad-number-1-7113941|title=Leeds Rhinos: Rising star awarded Rhinos’ final squad number|publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post|date=2015-02-19|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>

=== England ===
It was revealed on 16 October 2018, that Mulhern would be making his [[England national rugby league team|England]] début as an interchange against [[France national rugby league team|France]] on 17 October 2018, in a [[Test match (rugby league)|Test match]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]].<ref>{{cite web|date=16 October 2018|title=Mulhern earns England call-up|url=http://mobile.twitter.com/England_RL/status/1052138467264778240?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Etweet}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=16 October 2018|title=Mulhern is named on the bench for England début|url=http://www.rugby-league.com/article/53773/england-side-to-face-france-}}</ref>

[[England national rugby league team|England]] defeated the [[France national rugby league team|French]] 44-6, with Mulhern making an appearance from off the bench.<ref>{{cite web|date=17 October 2018|title=England defeat the French|url=http://mobile.twitter.com/England_RL/status/1052657932796682240}}</ref><ref name="Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2018/10/17/tom-johnstone-marks-debut-england-hat-trick-against-france/|title=Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France|publisher=Guardian|date=17 October 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref>

He won his second cap in Game 3 win over {{rlnt|Tonga}} of the [[2023 Tonga tour of England]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Nov 2023|title=Exclusive: Leigh Leopards powerhouse Robbie Mulhern makes ‘pretty cool’ history in England’s test series win over Tonga|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/exclusive-leigh-leopards-powerhouse-robbie-mulhern-makes-pretty-cool-history-in-englands-test-series-win-over-tonga}}</ref>

=== England Knights ===
Following Mulhern's exceptional season for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]],<ref>{{cite web|date=4 October 2018|title=Mulhern named Young Player of the Year|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/quinlan-dominates-2018-player-of-the-year-awards/}}</ref> it was revealed by the [[Rugby Football League]] on 24 July 2018, that Mulhern had been added to the 25-man [[England Knights]]' Performance Squad, that would be touring [[Papua New Guinea]] for a two-game [[Test match (rugby league)|Test match]] series later in the year.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref>

In 2018 he was selected for the [[England Knights]] on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]], and Mulhern would be linking-up with his then [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] teammate [[Chris Atkin]], who was already previously announced within the [[England Knights|Knights]]' fold.

Mulhern made his début for the [[England Knights]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] on 27 October 2018, the game played in [[Lae]] ended in a 12-16 victory to the [[England Knights|Knights]].<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref> He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea v England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea v England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>
